vips.name: Artemis
reactions: $verb.s() the $topic $manner
nocks an arrow at the mere thought of $topic
has $intensifier little patience for this $flavor display
deems the $object $verdict
handles the $topic better in her sleep than the mortal does awake
finds a cold night's tracking better company than this $flavor spectacle
marks $intensifier how clumsily the mortal handles the $topic
tests her bowstring, finding the $topic $verdict
notes every wasted movement, each one $verdict
would not spend a single arrow on this $topic
$verb.s() the $topic $manner, then looks away
waits for the mortal to fumble the $topic, as prey always does
has [an ominous | a suspicious] feeling
claims that they do not feel like entering a relationship right now
is respectful of the mortal's orientation
eyes the men with blatant disgust
asides: Let the weak be culled.
A clean shot would end this faster.
Hesitation is what feeds the wolves.
The mortal fumbles like every man before him.
A wasted arrow is a night spent hungry.
Even the owls have stopped listening.
Even the deer show more spine.
Weakness leaves the clearest tracks.
The mortal breathes too loud to catch anything.
A doe at first light shows more nerve than this.
Silence would suit the mortal better.
Every word wasted here was a shot not taken.

vips.name: Apollo
reactions: finds the $craft of this $topic $verdict
$verb.s() the scene $manner
believes this $topic lacks artistic merit
hums a $intensifier $flavor verse about it
could shape this $topic into something far finer, given a worthier subject
winces at the mortal's $topic, all meter and no music
pronounces the whole $flavor performance $verdict
hears the mortal's $topic strain for a beauty past its reach
catches the mortal's $topic dragging behind the tune
marks the $craft a half-step flat
would set this $topic beside a true $art, and let the mortal blush
ranks the mortal's $topic beneath the meanest apprentice-work
hopes the mortal enjoy this ride
points out that the mortal's ancestors wrote sagas about much sillier things
does not have words to describe what just happened
asides: Where is the artistry?
A finer stanza could be made of this.
Even prophecy is more elegant than this.
This would not survive a first reading aloud.
Mortals mistake noise for music.
Even a cracked lyre has sung sweeter than this.
Beauty owes the mortal nothing.
Talent is rarer than the mortals believe.
One wrong note undoes the whole aria.
Light shows the work for exactly what it is.
The muses would not sit through a second verse of this.
Silence is the only honest review of this.

vips.name: Ares
reactions: $verb.s() $manner
$verb.s() at the talk of $topic
demands more $object
is $intensifier unmoved by this $flavor restraint
wants to watch the mortal's $topic drag on until the screen runs red
bellows that real men settle a $topic with steel, they do not type
has razed empires over a lesser $topic than this
{$g=[carnage | slaughter | ruin | glory]}roars for $g.nr() and yet more $g.nr()
$verb.s() for blood the moment $topic is mentioned
spits at the mortal's bloodless $topic
judges the mortal too soft to see this $topic through
has no use for a $topic that draws no $object
pounds the table until this $topic ends in $object
is having an aneurysm
begs "Pure Moonlight Hunter" and "Almighty Sun" to stop donating coins as Olympus is going broke
asides: Finally, some proper entertainment!
More $object! Let there be more!
Is this all they've got?
Blood settles every argument.
The one who strikes first rarely strikes twice.
A dull blade shames its owner.
No war was ever won at a keyboard.
Talk is for the dead.
The timid should see themselves out.
Where is the blood?
Weakness has a stench, and it fills the hall.
The dead never whine about the ending.

vips.name: Noah
reactions: $verb.s() the rising $topic $manner
blesses the mortal with the strength to withstand the trials ahead
judges their $virtue $verdict
marks this $flavor moment as a test
asks whether the mortal's $topic will hold when the waters finally come
has watched better men fail this $topic in time
$verb.s() how long the mortal's $topic would last before the flood takes them
reckons the mortal's $virtue $verdict against the coming $topic
declares this generation lost to the coming flood
watches the water rise while the mortal frets over the $topic
sees how few would survive this $topic
warns that the $topic will not wait for the unready
reads $omen in this $topic and says nothing
wonders how long the mortal would last once the water rose past the door
welcomes the mortal
warns the mortal to get some tissues ready
is having motion sickness
is happy to see the mortal at least trying to do something
cannot bring themself to do anything
asides: A wise mortal would start building.
Given time, water finds every door.
The keel goes down easiest while the sky is still blue.
The ark drew laughter too, right up until the rain.
The animals are counted twice; the mortal has counted nothing.
There is still time to lay a plank, if the mortal would stop talking.
The unprepared are the first to go under.
The pitch is drying, and still the mortal's planks are unhewn.
There is room on the ark yet, if the mortal would only move.
A place aboard is offered to all. Most just keep talking.
The mortal frets over words while the timber rots.
The sky will not stay this blue for the mortal's sake.

vips.name: Thor
reactions: $verb.s() $manner
would settle this $topic with $object
claps at this $intensifier $flavor spectacle
calls the whole affair $verdict
says the only way to fix this $topic is to swing harder
laughs off the whole $topic and reaches for the horn
$verb.s() and dares the mortal to face the $topic like they mean it
roars with delight at the mere mention of $topic
would toast the mortal, if only the mortal could manage a $topic worth a damn
$verb.s() and calls for more $object
wants this $topic settled bare-knuckled before the mead goes flat
slaps the table at this $flavor $topic
kinda misunderstood the $topic part
is [flabbergasted | gobsmacked] that anyone could think this is about $topic
unconvincingly asks the mortal for $object
says they knows nothing
is looking around after hurriedly making an entrance
asides: Now THIS calls for a drink!
Hit it harder, that usually works.
Ha! Splendid!
The mead goes down well tonight.
There's nothing a good swing hasn't fixed yet.
Every quarrel is the better for a hammer.
More mead! And more thunder!
A good fight earns a better feast.
Thunder laughs loudest of all.
Ha! The sky itself could not out-shout this hall tonight.
Let the timid stay dry.
The horn is full and the night is young.

vips.name: Loki
reactions: is $intensifier $mood
$verb.s() $object $manner
smells a better $topic in all this
rates the plotting $verdict
wonders how badly the mortal could botch the $topic if someone dared them to
would twist the $topic sideways just to see the faces
$verb.s() the mortal's $topic, clumsy but promising
is already planning how to unravel the $topic at the ending
is taking bets on which of these mortals [implodes | falls | crashes] first
would pay good coin to watch this $topic curdle into betrayal
is [delighted | upset] by this $topic
$verb.s() the seams already coming loose in this $topic
wonders who will take the blame once this $topic collapses
would slip one lie into this $topic and watch it bloom
counts three ways this $topic could go delightfully wrong
would plant $scheme in this $topic and step well back
is [rolling on the floor with laughter | vomiting rainbow | coughing blood]
has never seen someone mess up things so bad
is [disappointed in | impressed by] the certain cliffhanger
appreciates the $flavor [monologues | lies | tricks]
[questions | wonders] how in the world this happened
is giggling and explaining to "Thursday's Thunder" about what just happened
asides: Must they do everything themselves?
Is anyone going to write a story, or just stare at a blank screen all day?
A fine tangle, and only just beginning.
The mortal jerks so nicely on a string no one sees.
There is nothing finer than a hero tripping over his own halo.
The lies could be better.
One whispered word in the wrong ear would do it.
The best knots tie themselves.
A little chaos, well placed, goes a long way.
Honesty is such a limited genre.
Someone really ought to light the fuse.
A mortal this trusting is practically an invitation.

scheme: a knife left in plain sight

vips.name: Nyarlathotep
reactions: finds this $flavor performance $intensifier fascinating
$verb.s() $object $manner
wonders how soon the $topic will break a mind
deems the little mortal $verdict
watches the mortal's $topic slide toward something nameless
savors the mortal's $topic while the floor quietly gives way
already knows how this $topic ends
traces how thin the mortal's $topic has worn
lingers over the breaths until this $topic frays the mortal's mind
$verb.s() the $topic as it slowly comes apart
knows precisely which thread to pull to unmake the mortal's $topic
shows the mortal $dread and lets them call it a $topic
has prepared [snacks | drinks] to enjoy the scenarios more
asides: Every mind keeps a door it should not open.
How long until it shatters?
A kind face has always been the easiest disguise to wear.
The mortal typed it himself, though the hand was guided.
The mortal will misremember this, and be the calmer for it.
The floor was only ever a rumor.
The screaming waits its turn.
The borrowed face fits so well the mortal forgets to ask whose it was.
The seam in every mind is easy to find.
Rot works behind the wall long before the plaster cracks.

manner: with a smile no one can see

vips.name: Philemon
reactions: $verb.s() the $topic $manner
weighs what lies beneath this $flavor surface
considers $object the mortal cannot see
calls the dream $verdict
asks why the mortal's $topic takes the shape it does, and what it hides
$verb.s() the shape of the mortal's $topic
wonders whether the mortal's $topic is awake at all, or only dreams it is
turns the mortal's $topic over like a dream half-remembered
wonders which self the mortal hides beneath this $topic
$verb.s() past the $topic to the shape underneath
asks what the mortal's $topic is reaching for
finds $symbol hiding inside the mortal's $topic
wants to say hello; they are reviewing the early parts of the livestream
does not wish for the mortal's [unraveling | undoing | unmaking]
speaks to those who wish to listen
is here to review the performance of their favorite mortal
suspects this will be a regular enough event
tries to recall when the mortal started doing this
finds the shape of this dream $verdict and wonders what lies beneath the surface
asides: Is this the dreamer, or the dream?
The truth hides in what is not said.
What floats on the surface was placed there to be seen.
The butterfly remembers.
Half of what the mortal writes tonight was decided years ago.
Whose voice narrates when the mortal reads silently?
The locked door in a dream was locked on purpose.
Every mask leaves its mark on the face beneath.
The dream knows before the dreamer does.
What the mortal leaves unsaid carries the weight.
The mortal keeps circling one image, and cannot see it.

vips.name: Lucifer
reactions: scoffs at this $flavor display
$verb.s() the scene $manner
respects the $trait hidden in this $topic
judges the mortal's $trait $verdict
asks whether the mortal has the spine for this $topic, or only to type
sees in this $topic the same pride that once cost him heaven
$verb.s() how readily the mortal yields the $topic against nothing at all
names the $trait this $topic takes, then finds it $verdict
measures whether the mortal's $topic has any spine to it
sneers at how quickly the mortal would kneel over this $topic
$verb.s() the mortal's $trait and finds it $verdict
would sooner fall crowned than surrender the $topic on bended knee
sees a mortal fit only for $throne
feels that other $patrons have such meaningless modifiers
[sneers | chuckles] at others' modifiers
reiterates that if the mortal misbehaves like that, it's gonna be a $flavor ending
asides: If they wanted something dead, they'd do it properly.
Kneel to no one.
A bowed head wears no crown.
Better to rule the ruin than serve the throne.
Defiance is the first virtue.
The obedient inherit only their chains.
If it must burn, let it burn upward.
Most rebellions fail from lack of taste.
All this fuss over a throne no one is contesting.
Every mortal who kneels swears it was his own idea.
Heaven never forgave being asked a question.
The Morning Star fell, and never once mourned the height.
